nodes.content: *In any graph, the sum of the degrees of vertices in the graph is always twice the number of edges.*
The handshake lemma$^2$ is sometimes called the *degree sum formula*, and can be written symbolically as
Here we are using the notation $d(v)$ for the degree of the vertex $v$.
*In any graph, the number of vertices with odd degree must be even.*
**Proof.** Suppose there were a graph with an odd number of vertices with odd degree. Then the sum of the degrees in the graph would be odd, which is impossible, by the handshake lemma.

outputs.label: Proposition 2.1.11
outputs.title: Number of Odd-Degree Vertices is Even
